Name: Caterpie
Ability-Effect: At the end of your opponent's turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

Name: Metapod
Flavor-Text: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

Name: Cacnea
Flavor-Text: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

Name: Tropius
Flavor-Text: Delicious fruits grew out from around its neck because it always ate the same kind of fruit.

Name: Petilil
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If the leaves on its head are pruned with regularity, this Pokémon can be grown into a fine plump shape.

Name: Hisuian Lilligant
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

Name: Ninetales
Effects: Flip 9 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It has nine long tails and fur that gleams gold. It is said to live for 1,000 years.

Name: Growlithe
Effects: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: It’s very friendly and faithful to people. It will try to repel enemies by barking and biting.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If its chronic headache peaks, it may exhibit odd powers. It seems unable to recall such an episode.

Name: Goldeen
Flavor-Text: Its dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.

Name: Seaking
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.

Name: Feebas
Flavor-Text: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms schools and lives at the bottom of rivers that teem with plant life.

Name: Milotic ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may take a [W] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to this Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Snorunt
Flavor-Text: It is said that a home visited by a Snorunt will prosper. It can withstand cold of −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Froslass
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you can't, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It freezes prey by blowing its −58 degree Fahrenheit breath. It is said to then secretly display its prey.

Name: Luvdisc
Flavor-Text: During the spawning season, countless Luvdisc congregate at coral reefs, turning the waters pink.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

Name: Iron Bundle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

Name: Pikachu
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Raichu
Flavor-Text: If the electric pouches in its cheeks become fully charged, both ears will stand straight up.

Name: Emolga
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries. Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.

Name: Dedenne ex
Effects: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Yamper
Effects: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

Name: Munna
Flavor-Text: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it’s eating a good dream, and black when it’s eating a nightmare.

Name: Musharna
Flavor-Text: It drowses and dreams all the time. It’s best to leave it be if it’s just woken up, as it’s a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

Name: Sylveon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, you may heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents’ weak spots.

Name: Carbink
Effects: Choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, take a [P] Energy from your Energy Zone and attach it to that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that somewhere in the world, there’s a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering Carbink. It’s also said that this pack has a queen.

Name: Mega Diancie ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Enamorus
Effects: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have 1 or more of the same type of Energy attached, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When it flies to this land from across the sea, the bitter winter comes to an end. According to legend, this Pokémon’s love gives rise to the budding of fresh life across Hisui.

Name: Fidough
Effects: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: The yeast in Fidough’s breath is useful for cooking, so this Pokémon has been protected by people since long ago.

Name: Flutter Mane
Effects: If this Pokémon didn't move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.

Name: Wooper
Flavor-Text: When it walks around on land, it coats its body with a slimy, poisonous film.

Name: Quagsire
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks is chosen at random. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use the chosen attack.
Flavor-Text: Its body is always slimy. It often bangs its head on the river bottom as it swims but seems not to care.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Reveal all of your Pokémon in play and in your hand that have the Puppy Pile attack, and this attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon you revealed in this way.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon travels in a pack with others until it grows up. When its mood turns sour, it starts striking the ground with the rocks on its neck.

Name: Sandygast
Flavor-Text: It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they’re blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.

Name: Palossand
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones of its victims, which are all dried up after being drained of their vitality.

Name: Mega Sableye ex
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack, do 40 damage to the Attacking Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has damage on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in deserts. It becomes active at night when it hunts for prey exhausted from the desert’s heat.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It was formed by uniting 108 spirits. It has been bound to the Odd Keystone to keep it from doing any mischief.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: It will abruptly headbutt anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is extremely hard.

Name: Scrafty
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon may be rude, but it takes very good care of its family, its friends, and its turf.

Name: Mareanie
Effects: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has excellent regenerative capabilities. Legs that a Mareanie has lost have a very bitter, astringent flavor—they’re not suitable as food.

Name: Toxapex
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Do 40 damage to this Pokémon instead of the usual amount for this Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: Toxapex gets into fierce battles with Bruxish over areas where warm ocean currents flow, but the odds are always against it.

Name: Goomy
Flavor-Text: Goomy’s horns are excellent sensory organs that cover all five of Goomy’s senses. Goomy picks up on danger from the movement of the air.

Name: Hisuian Sliggoo
Flavor-Text: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui’s water.

Name: Hisuian Goodra
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon takes −80 damage from that attack.
Flavor-Text: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If it inflates to sing a lullaby, it can perform longer and cause sure drowsiness in its audience.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Flavor-Text: The rich, fluffy fur that covers its body feels so good that anyone who feels it can’t stop touching it.

Name: Eevee
Flavor-Text: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element stones.

Name: Munchlax
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When it is voraciously gulping down food, it forgets about the food it has hidden under its fur.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

Name: Teddiursa
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, attacks used by the Defending Pokémon do −20 damage.
Flavor-Text: Its paws are soaked in oodles of honey. When nervous, Teddiursa will lick its paws and soon have a smile back on its face.

Name: Ursaring
Flavor-Text: It is quite skilled at climbing trees. If it comes across a Primeape while searching for berries in the treetops, trouble will surely ensue.

Name: Ursaluna
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.
Flavor-Text: I believe it was Hisui’s swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.

Name: Hisuian Zorua
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

Name: Hisuian Zoroark ex
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Furfrou
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes −20 damage from attacks.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Skwovet
Flavor-Text: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

Name: Greedent
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

Name: Small Balloon
Description: The Retreat Cost of the Basic Pokémon this card is attached to is 1 less.

Name: Elegant Cape
Description: The Stage 1 Pokémon this card is attached to gets +30 HP.

Name: Elesa
Description: Return all Pokémon Tools attached to each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) to their owner's hand.

Name: Puppy-Loving Girl
Description: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck. Put all Pokémon you find there that have the Puppy Pile attack into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

Name: Wallace
Description: Choose 1 of your [W] Pokémon in play with a maximum HP of 50 or less. Put a random [W] Pokémon from your deck that evolves from that Pokémon onto that Pokémon to evolve it.

Name: Kid's Room
Description: Once during each player's turn, that player may choose a card in their hand and switch it with a random Pokémon Tool card in their deck.

Name: Vulpix
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: As its body grows larger, its six warm tails become more beautiful, with a more luxurious coat of fur.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It doesn’t like to be taken care of. It’s difficult to bond with since it won’t listen to its Trainer.

Name: Pikachu
Flavor-Text: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can build and cause lightning storms.

Flavor-Text: Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents’ weak spots.

Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Caterpie
Ability-Effect: At the end of your opponent's turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, put a random card from your deck that evolves from this Pokémon onto this Pokémon to evolve it.
Flavor-Text: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.

Name: Metapod
Flavor-Text: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.

Name: Butterfree
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.

Name: Mareep
Flavor-Text: Its fleece grows continually. In the summer, the fleece is fully shed, but it grows back in a week.

Name: Flaaffy
Flavor-Text: Because of its rubbery, electricity-resistant skin, it can store lots of electricity in its fur.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random. This attack also does 20 damage to it.
Flavor-Text: When it gets dark, the light from its bright, shiny tail can be seen from far away on the ocean’s surface.

Name: Slowbro
Effects: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

Name: Kangaskhan
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.

Name: Ditto
Effects: Choose 1 of your Benched Pokémon's attacks, except any Pokémon ex, and use it as this attack. If this Pokémon doesn't have the necessary Energy to use that attack, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It can freely recombine its own cellular structure to transform into other life-forms.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Stadium cards from their hand.
Flavor-Text: Its stomach’s digestive juices can dissolve any kind of poison. Eating things off the ground doesn’t bother it at all.

Name: Mega Gyarados ex
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Vaporeon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Mega Ampharos ex
Effects: 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon is chosen at random 3 times. For each time a Pokémon was chosen, also do 20 damage to it.
Ex-Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 3 points.

Name: Indeedee ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.
Ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent gets 2 points.

Name: Munchlax
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: When it is voraciously gulping down food, it forgets about the food it has hidden under its fur.

Name: Small Balloon
Description: The Retreat Cost of the Basic Pokémon this card is attached to is 1 less.
